system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,T)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss upwind;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

keyword T0 is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/BernardCells/3/constant/physicalProperties/mixture/equationOfState"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/BernardCells/3/constant/physicalProperties/mixture/equationOfState from line 25 to line 26.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword T0 is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties/mixture/equationOfState"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver expects a `T0` (reference temperature) keyword inside the `equationOfState` sub-dictionary of `physicalProperties`. Previous attempts added `rho0`, but now the solver is asking for `T0`.

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file, the `equationOfState` sub-dictionary contains `type Boussinesq` and `rho0 1`, but it's missing `T0`. For the Boussinesq equation of state in `buoyantFoam`, the solver requires `T0` to compute the thermal expansion coefficient and handle the buoyancy coupling.

## Root Cause

The Boussinesq equation of state implementation in OpenFOAM 10's `buoyantFoam` solver requires a `T0` entry within the `equationOfState` sub-dictionary. This represents the reference temperature used for the linear approximation of density variation with temperature.

## Proposed Solution

Add the `T0` keyword inside the `equationOfState` sub-dictionary within the `mixture` block of `physicalProperties`. The `equationOfState` sub-dictionary should include:

```
equationOfState
{
    type        Boussinesq;
    rho0        1;
    T0          300;
}
```

So the complete `mixture` block should be:

```
mixture
{
    equationOfState
    {
        type        Boussinesq;
        rho0        1;
        T0          300;
    }

    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }

    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1007;
        Hf          0;
    }

    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}
```

The key additions are `rho0 1;` and `T0 300;`. The `T0` value of 300 K serves as the reference temperature for the Boussinesq approximation. Since one wall is at 310 K and the other at 300 K, setting `T0` to 300 K makes sense as the reference temperature.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/physicalProperties', changes='Add T0 300 inside the equationOfState sub-dictionary under mixture, alongside the existing rho0 1 entry')]
